title_tesim: Dr. Cass Arthur Reed, and others [Pomona college?], [Claremont?], 1928
caption_tesim: Hndwrittenon the bottom of the negative: 70 P2
Text from negative sleeve: 2369 P1 to 9, 2370 P1 to 5, Near East Colleges, Dr. Cass A. Reed, Mrs. A.L. Bagnall, 951 Western, also Claremont -Pomona College
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: Dr. Cass Arthur Reed was a graduate of Pomona College, and then later, Harvard, Columbia and the Union Theological Seminary. He earned degrees from all of these institutions. In 1912, he was appointed the Dean of the International College of Smyrna Turkey. He is referenced in numerous L.A. Times articles, including the Aug. 11, 1914 article "Pomona 'Grad' College Dean Home from Smyrna, Will Take Sports Leader with Him to Turkey".

This image is of Dr. Cass Arthur Reed, wearing a doctoral cap and gown, made evident by the three velvet bars on each sleeve of the gown. In his hand is a diploma or award of some kind, with the word "Colligii" legible. The other half of the document is held by another man, also in doctoral dress. Other men, also in doctoral dress, surround them. This photograph may have been taken at Pomona College on 28 Jan. 1928 when Dr. Reed was given the honorary degree of doctor of divinity.

title_tesim: Judith Evans from "O.K. By Me" directed by Louise Glaum, Los Angeles, 1952.
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: Louise Glaum, 1952, O.K. by Me, Play, Louise Glaum.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: This production was referenced by two Los Angeles Times articles, "Stage Farce Offered at Beaux Arts Theater." 11 Oct. 1952, and "Beaux Arts Will Reopen." 19 Sep. 1952.

Louise Glaum was the director of this production of "O.K. By Me," a play by Sheldon Shepard. Mrs. Glaum was an actress, drama teacher and director, who was active in the Los Angeles area for over 60 years. Originally known as one of the first "vamp" actresses, she later opened several theaters in Los Angeles and an acting school.
This image is of a Judith Evans from the play "O.K. By Me," wearing a dark off the shoulder dress. 1952.

title_tesim: Section from the north frieze of the Parthenon with horsemen created in ca. 438-432 BCE, copy print 1962
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: Marbles. Athens Acropolis. Now in British Museum. Copy Life Mag. from a magazine story. 1962.
Photograph caption: Horsemen -- These mounted Athenians are from the north frieze. The Elgin Marbles have taught us much of how the ancient Greeks looked, dressed and fought.

funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: This frieze is part of the Elgin Marbles collection at the British Museum; originally located on the Parthenon in Athens. The frieze was created by the workshop of Phidias (ancient Greek sculptor, ca. 500-ca. 432 BCE).
Copy print of a section of the north "cavalcade" frieze from the Parthenon

caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: Elmer Pond Family, Parents 60th Wed. anniversary, Santa Monica June 1, 1953. 307 Washington St, at Third St. Parents Mr. & Mrs. Larkin Pond, Compton, Calif.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: This image is of the Pond family on Larkin an Nancy Pond's 60th wedding anniversary. In the front slightly to the right of the photo are Mr. and Mrs. Larkin M. Pond. Likely present in the photo is their son, Elmer Pond, and Elmer's wife, Elizabeth Pond, among others.

title_tesim: Dr. Cass Arthur Reed [rephotographed]
caption_tesim: Text from negative sleeve: 2369 P1 to 9, 2370 P1 to 5, Near East Colleges, Dr. Cass A. Reed, Mrs. A.L. Bagnall, 951 Western, also Claremont -Pomona College.
Handwritten on the edge of the negative:  2370 P6. Cass Arthur Reed.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections
funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
description_tesim: Dr. Cass Arthur Reed was a graduate of Pomona College, and then later, Harvard, Columbia and the Union Theological Seminary. He earned degrees from all of these institutions. In 1912, he was appointed the Dean of the International College of Smyrna Turkey. He is referenced in numerous L.A. Times articles, including the Aug. 11, 1914 article "Pomona 'Grad' College Dean Home from Smyrna, Will Take Sports Leader with Him to Turkey".
This image appears to be a rephotograph of Dr. Reed, made evident by the inscription "Underwood & Underwood, Studio N.Y." visible in the bottom right corner of the image.

funding_note_tesim: Access to this collection is generously supported by Arcadia funds.
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Adelbert Bartlett Papers. Department of Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.
